files_changed: backend/app/lib/model.py (added CALIBRATOR_PATH, get_calibrator(), reload_calibrator(); predict() applies calibrator)
backend/scripts/train_calibrator.py (NEW — fits calibrator on time-aware holdout, persists to calibrator.pkl)
motivation: When the model says '73% no-go', that should actually mean ~73% of historical days that scored 0.73 were no-go days. Most tree models and LSTM raw sigmoids are NOT well calibrated — they tend to be over-confident at the extremes. Phase 7 fixes this with a post-hoc calibrator trained on the time-aware holdout.

lstm_path_attempted.note: LSTM trained on 110 samples (49 positive); predictions on holdout were ANTI-calibrated (AUC 0.211, ECE 0.300). This is consistent with the Phase 3 tier gate (LSTM_MIN_SAMPLES=500, LSTM_MIN_AUC=0.65) which correctly rejects this model — and is exactly why the live model.selected_tier() falls back to rules. The calibrator was therefore fitted on the rules path, where the 28-sample holdout has only 3 unique probability values (0.10, 0.45, 0.85) and falls below the auto() minimum-sample threshold.

calibrator_choice.reason: Holdout size (28) is below the auto() minimum (30), and the source probability has only 3 unique values (0.10/0.45/0.85) — Platt cannot fit a meaningful logistic on that and the empirical-bin isotonic would overfit. Returning identity is the honest answer; the infrastructure is in place and will engage automatically once labels cross the LSTM_MIN_SAMPLES=500 threshold (Phase 3).

infrastructure_added.auto_selection_logic: Platt vs isotonic — lower Brier wins; falls back to identity on <30 samples or degenerate univariate input
infrastructure_added.persistence: pickled to backend/data/calibrator.pkl via Calibrator.save / Calibrator.load
infrastructure_added.integration: model.predict() applies Calibrator.predict(raw_p) before returning p_bad. model_version() appends [cal-identity|platt|isotonic] so operators can see calibration state at a glance.

calibration_strategy_when_more_data_arri.phase_3_threshold: Once LSTM_MIN_SAMPLES=500 is met, the LSTM path engages and produces continuous probabilities — Platt then becomes meaningful.
calibration_strategy_when_more_data_arri.platt_vs_isotonic_choice: Auto() picks the lower-Brier winner. Isotonic needs >=100 samples to avoid overfitting; below that we use Platt only.
calibration_strategy_when_more_data_arri.retraining_cadence: Re-run scripts/train_calibrator.py after every scripts/train_model.py invocation. Add to the operator's standard retrain loop.
calibration_strategy_when_more_data_arri.monitoring: ECE is logged in Calibrator.metrics and surfaced via the calibrator.version_string() baked into model_version.

key_phase7_tests: test_auto_uses_empirical_bin_for_univariate_input — rules with 3 unique probs => empirical isotonic
test_save_and_load_roundtrip — pickle preserves method + predict() output
next_phase: Phase 8 — Active-learning UI loop. When the model is uncertain (p_bad in 0.35–0.65), surface a 'confirm yesterday's conditions?' nudge on the dashboard so operators fill the highest-value gaps in the label corpus.
